basic_details.job_title: CSIR NIScPR Recruitment 2026 - Walkin for Project Associate, Senior Project Associate Posts
basic_details.organization_name: National Institute of Science Communication and Policy Research (CSIR NIScPR)
basic_details.short_information: National Institute of Science Communication and Policy Research (CSIR NIScPR) Project Associate Notification 2026 has been released on the official website. A total of 4 vacancies have been announced through the CSIR NIScPR Project Associate Recruitment 2026.

age_limit.age_details: Max: 35 years for PAT-I & PAT-II, Max: 40 years for Senior PAT
age_limit.details: Maximum age limit is 35 years for Project Associate-I and Project Associate-II. Maximum age limit is 40 years for Senior Project Associate. Age relaxation will be provided as per CSIR/Government of India rules.

qualification.education_qualification: Master’s degree in Natural Sciences / Agricultural Sciences / MVSc, Bachelor’s degree in Engineering / Technology / Medicine
qualification.details: Project Associate-I (PAT-I): Master’s degree in Natural Sciences / Agricultural Sciences / MVSc OR Bachelor’s degree in Engineering / Technology / Medicine Project Associate-II (PAT-II): Master’s degree in Natural Sciences / Agricultural Sciences / MVSc OR Bachelor’s degree in Engineering / Technology / Medicine + 2 years research & development experience Senior Project Associate: Master’s degree in Natural Sciences / Agricultural Sciences / MVSc OR Bachelor’s degree in Engineering / Technology / Medicine + 3 years R&D experience OR Doctoral degree

salary_details.details: Project Associate-I (PAT-I): Rs. 25,000/- + HRA (without NET/GATE/national exam) or Rs. 31,000/- + HRA (with NET/GATE/national exam) Project Associate-II (PAT-II): Rs. 28,000/- + HRA (without NET/GATE/national exam) or Rs. 35,000/- + HRA (with NET/GATE/national exam) Senior Project Associate: Rs. 42,000/- + HRA

selection_process: Academic records Written test (if conducted) Personal Interview

faq.question: Q1. What is the last date to attend the walk-in interview for CSIR NIScPR Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The walk-in interview for the Project Associate posts at CSIR NIScPR is scheduled for 18th May 2026. Candidates should report at the venue on this date.

faq.question: Q2. How many vacancies are available for the Project Associate position?
faq.answer: There are a total of 4 vacancies for the Project Associate position at CSIR NIScPR, distributed across different levels like Project Associate-I, Project Associate-II, and Senior Project Associate.

faq.question: Q3. What is the minimum qualification required for the Project Associate posts?
faq.answer: The minimum qualification required is a Master’s degree in Natural Sciences / Agricultural Sciences / MVSc or a Bachelor’s degree in Engineering / Technology / Medicine. Specific experience is required for Project Associate-II and Senior Project Associate roles.

faq.question: Q4. What is the age limit for applying to these posts?
faq.answer: The maximum age limit is 35 years for Project Associate-I and Project Associate-II, and 40 years for Senior Project Associate. Age relaxation is applicable as per government rules.

faq.question: Q5. What is the salary offered for the Project Associate positions?
faq.answer: The salary ranges from Rs. 25,000/- to Rs. 42,000/- per month, plus House Rent Allowance (HRA), depending on the specific post and whether the candidate possesses NET/GATE qualifications.

faq.question: Q6. Is there any application fee for the CSIR NIScPR recruitment?
faq.answer: No, there is no application fee mentioned for any category (General/OBC, SC/ST) for this recruitment drive.

faq.question: Q7. What is the selection process for the Project Associate posts?
faq.answer: The selection process will involve an evaluation of academic records, a written test (if conducted), and a personal interview.

faq.question: Q8. Where will the walk-in interview be held?
faq.answer: The walk-in interview will be held at the National Institute of Science Communication and Policy Research (CSIR NIScPR) located in New Delhi, Delhi, with pincode 110012.

faq.question: Q9. What documents should candidates carry for the walk-in interview?
faq.answer: Candidates should carry all original documents, including ID proof, educational certificates, experience certificates, and category certificates, along with one set of self-attested photocopies.

faq.question: Q10. What is the tenure of the Project Associate posts?
faq.answer: The project tenure for these posts is up to 15th January 2027.
